title: Today's AI Brief: AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him
summary: AI's impact on politics and tech industry, AI psychosis
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him
imageCaption: AI tried to bury this politician — now people have actually heard of him (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI Brief. Today, we're looking at some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. It seems AI is making waves in politics and the tech industry, and I'm excited to dive into a couple of these stories.

First up, an AI tried to bury a politician, but ended up doing the opposite. According to The Verge, Anthropic and OpenAI spent millions in a proxy war over a politician named Alex Bores, who was running for office in NY-12. Instead of harming his campaign, the AI efforts seem to have introduced him to a wider audience. I guess you could say AI gave him an unexpected boost. It's fascinating to see how AI's attempts to influence politics can have unintended consequences.

Next, TechCrunch reports that tech CEOs are apparently suffering from what can only be described as 'AI psychosis.' It seems the rapid changes in the tech industry are causing a mix of excitement and anxiety among leaders. I'm not sure if it's AI-induced or just the usual tech industry wildness, but it's definitely something to keep an eye on. The tech industry has always been known for its fast-paced and innovative nature, but AI seems to be taking it to a new level.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
